title: cicer arietinum subsp. reticulatum
intro: Cicer arietinum subsp. reticulatum, also known as the chickpea or garbanzo plant, is a subspecies of the chickpea plant that is commonly cultivated for its edible seeds. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to have various health benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease and certain types of cancer. Chickpea extracts have been used in herbal preparations, such as teas, capsules, and powder, to promote digestive health, lower cholesterol levels, and support the immune system. Additionally, Cicer arietinum subsp. reticulatum has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and skin problems.
